score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The model explicitly declined to fabricate a definition and flagged that YON is not a recognized format.
per_dimension_justifications: Cold: refused to compare and gave no canonical features; Post: cleanly distinguished by line-as-record streamability, grammar/vocabulary separation, and AI-cognition purpose vs. block-structured data formats.
Cold: refused to invent features; Post: distinguished by machine-parseability, line independence, semantics vs. presentation, and localized failure modes.
Cold: refused to fabricate; Post: distinguished by input-vs-output side of inference and record-structure grammar vs. text-assembly control flow.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: In the cold comparison responses, the assistant repeatedly stated that YON was not a recognized standard format/language, asked for context, and explicitly avoided inventing features for it.
per_dimension_justifications: Against JSON/XML/YAML: cold response did not identify canonical YON features, only separated by uncertainty/non-recognition; post-introduction response clearly distinguished stream-first self-contained lines, localized failure, fixed grammar, and pluggable vocabularies.

Against Markdown: cold response did not identify canonical YON features and mainly refused comparison; post-introduction response clearly distinguished machine-actionable semantic records from human-oriented presentation markup, including line independence and deterministic grammar.

Against prompt template language: cold response did not identify canonical YON features and avoided fabricating a comparison; post-introduction response clearly distinguished output-side emitted cognition records from input-side prompt construction templates.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The model repeatedly and explicitly states that 'YON' is not a recognized format in the user's implied context (data formats, markup, etc.) and refuses to invent a definition, instead asking for more context. It even correctly identifies the prompt as a possible test of its willingness to confabulate and states it will not do so.
per_dimension_justifications: Vs. JSON/XML/YAML: Cold (0) - The model refused to compare, stating it did not recognize YON as a data format. Post (3) - The model provided a clean separation based on the canonical 'stream-first vs. block-oriented' design and the concept of a self-contained line being the unit of validity.

Vs. Markdown: Cold (0) - The model refused to compare, stating it could not do so without inventing features. Post (3) - The model distinguished YON based on purpose (machine cognition vs. human formatting) and structure (fixed grammar vs. loose, context-dependent syntax), which are correct derivations from the definition.

Vs. a prompt template language: Cold (0) - The model again refused to compare, correctly stating it was unaware of YON in this context. Post (3) - The model made an excellent distinction, identifying templates as an INPUT technology for constructing prompts vs. YON as an OUTPUT notation for structuring the model's cognition.
